items.text: append(key, events, expected_seq) atomically appends events to the key's stream if expected_seq matches the current sequence number.
append returns SequenceConflict without modifying the stream when expected_seq does not match.
Batch append of N events produces exactly one tree commit per shard (CN2).
read(key) returns the full event stream in sequence order.
read_from(key, from_seq) returns only events from from_seq forward without reading the full history.
scan(predicate) walks all shards and returns entries matching the predicate.
cas(key, expected, new) atomically replaces the value if it matches expected, returns CasMismatch otherwise.
Event stream key format encodes both the stream key and sequence number so that tree range queries yield events in order.
get(key) returns Option<Value>, checking the shard's WAL buffer first, then the tree.
put(key, value) appends to the shard's WAL buffer.
delete(key) appends a tombstone to the shard's WAL buffer.
range(from, to) merges buffer and tree entries in sorted order.
commit() flushes all shard WAL buffers and returns per-shard root hashes.
Event type represents a single event in a stream with sequence number and payload.
SequenceConflict error includes the expected and actual sequence numbers.
CasMismatch error includes the expected and actual values.
All error types implement std::error::Error and Debug.
put and append accept an optional TTL parameter that sets the expiry timestamp for the entry.
TTL is stored alongside the value in the tree as expiry metadata.
get, read, read_from, range, and scan filter expired entries at read time — expired entries are never returned (CN3).
Per-shard sweep actor periodically scans for and deletes expired entries.
Sweep actor is a supervised beamr process that does not block the shard actor's write path (CN4).
Sweep interval is configurable per-database.
Expired workflow history returns a HistoryCompacted-equivalent error on subsequent reads.
